description: Styling options for function plots: color, stroke width, dashed lines, and bounded range shading. For inequality fills use inequalityplot().
utility: Style a plot with color, fill, opacity, or domain restriction

arguments.name: equation
arguments.description: Function expression as a quoted ASCII math string, a function reference, or a string variable

arguments.name: range
arguments.description: Optional domain restriction for explicit plots (x range)

arguments.name: fillColor
arguments.description: Fill color for polar plots - e.g., fc(yellow)

arguments.name: fillOpacity
arguments.description: Fill opacity for inequality plots - e.g., fo(0.3)

alternates.description: Shade the region satisfying the inequality. Use inequalityplot() for inequality regions
Plot with a custom stroke color

examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(4,3), 14, 14, type(plotting))
inequalityplot(G, "y < x^2 + 1", c(orange), fo(0.3))

G = g2d(at(2,3), 30, 30, range(-2, 2, 0.5), range(-2, 2, 1), type(plotting))
plot(G, "x^2")
plot(G, "-x^2")
plot(G, "(x^2)sin(1/x)")
